name: Browser Pool Manager
description: Manages Playwright browser lifecycle and context pool. Provides a single Chromium browser instance with the ability to create multiple isolated browser contexts for concurrent page operations. Supports async context manager protocol for automatic resource cleanup.

types.name: BrowserPool

fields.name: _config
fields.description: Configuration containing viewport dimensions and capture settings

fields.name: _playwright
fields.description: Playwright instance, None before start() is called

fields.name: _browser
fields.description: Chromium browser instance, None before start() is called

types.description: Manages a single Playwright browser instance with multiple contexts. Supports async context manager protocol for automatic lifecycle management.

functions.name: __init__
functions.description: Initialize BrowserPool with configuration. Sets up internal state with null browser and playwright instances.

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: Configuration object containing viewport dimensions and other capture settings

preconditions: config must be a valid CaptureConfig instance

functions.name: start
functions.description: Launch the Playwright browser instance. Starts Playwright and launches a headless Chromium browser.
postconditions: _browser is a launched Chromium browser in headless mode

structured_side_effects.description: May download browser binaries if not cached

functions.name: new_context
functions.description: Create a new browser context with configured viewport and optional authentication. Each context is an isolated browsing session. HTTPS errors are ignored for all contexts.

inputs.name: auth
inputs.description: Optional authentication manager to apply credentials to the context

error_cases.name: ContextCreationFailure
error_cases.condition: Browser fails to create new context

preconditions: _browser is not None (start() must be called first)
postconditions: Returns a new isolated BrowserContext with configured viewport
If auth provided and has_auth=True, authentication is applied to context

structured_side_effects.description: Creates new browser context in the browser instance

functions.name: stop
functions.description: Close the browser and stop Playwright. Idempotent - safe to call multiple times. Cleans up all resources.
postconditions: _browser is closed and set to None
_playwright is stopped and set to None
All browser contexts are implicitly closed

structured_side_effects.description: Closes browser, stops playwright, nulls both fields

functions.name: __aenter__
functions.description: Async context manager entry. Starts the browser and returns self.
postconditions: Browser is started (start() has been called)
Returns self for use in async with statement

functions.name: __aexit__
functions.description: Async context manager exit. Stops the browser and cleans up resources regardless of whether an exception occurred.

inputs.name: args
inputs.description: Exception information (exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb) passed by context manager protocol

postconditions: Browser is stopped (stop() has been called)

structured_side_effects.description: Calls stop() which closes browser and playwright

invariants: If _browser is not None, then _playwright is not None
After start() completes successfully, both _playwright and _browser are non-None
After stop() completes, both _playwright and _browser are None
All contexts use viewport dimensions from _config
Browser is always launched in headless mode
requires: Playwright must be installed and browser binaries available
System must support running headless Chromium
CaptureConfig must have viewport_width and viewport_height attributes

data_access.rationale: Manages in-memory browser lifecycle state. No persistent storage operations.

side_effects.rationale: Launches and manages Chromium browser process via Playwright
